evals.prompt: I want to create a landing page for an Automotive parts dealer, which shows some informatino about the company and all of its products. Can you help me plan this before building it?
evals.expected_output: The skill infers this is a Simple-tier project (a single marketing page, no backend/auth/data-persistence concerns), runs a short conversational elicitation (4-7 questions per questions/simple.md), then produces a spec.md at the user's project location following templates/spec.template.md's Simple-tier sections (outcome, in scope, out of scope, constraints, requirements in SHALL/WHEN/IF form, acceptance criteria), gets it confirmed, then produces a flat tasks.md. No plan.md should be generated at this tier.
expectations: The skill selects the Simple tier for this project (not Medium or Complex), per tiers.md's criteria for a single marketing page with no backend/auth/data-persistence concerns
No plan.md is generated — Simple tier skips it entirely per tiers.md

spec.md includes exactly the Simple-tagged sections from spec.template.md: outcome, in scope, out of scope, constraints, prior decisions (if applicable), requirements, acceptance criteria — and omits medium+/complex-only sections (users & context, data & integrations, risks & open questions)
spec.md's requirements section uses EARS-style syntax: 'The system SHALL...' for always-active, 'WHEN..., the system SHALL...' for event-driven, 'IF..., the system SHALL...' for unwanted behavior
No section in spec.md is left with unfilled {{placeholder}} text
tasks.md is a flat checklist with no phase headers (phases are Complex-tier only)
Each task in tasks.md has an Acceptance line and does not need a plan.md Refs citation (since no plan.md exists at this tier)
The elicitation transcript shows roughly 4-7 distinct questions asked, not a much longer list
The transcript shows at least one explicit checkpoint (e.g. 'does this spec look right?') before tasks.md is generated
spec.md and tasks.md are written to the user's project directory, not into the skill's own folder
